---
name: Pull Request
about: Submit a pull request for review
---

## Description

Briefly describe the changes made in this pull request. What issue does this address?

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Code refactor
- [ ] Test update
- [ ] Other (please describe)

## Related Issues

Closes #(issue number)
Related to #(issue number)

## Changes Made

List the specific changes made in this PR:

- [ ] Change 1
- [ ] Change 2
- [ ] Change 3

Include file paths modified and a brief description of each change.

## Testing

Describe the testing performed for these changes:

- [ ] Unit tests pass locally
- [ ] Integration tests pass locally
- [ ] Manual testing completed
- [ ] New tests added for this change
- [ ] All existing tests still pass

### Test Environment

- **OS**: [e.g. Windows 10, macOS 12.0, Ubuntu 22.04]
- **Node.js Version**: [e.g. 18.17.0]
- **Database**: [e.g. PostgreSQL 14.5]

## Screenshots (if applicable)

If this PR includes UI changes, please include screenshots:

**Before:**
[Screenshot]

**After:**
[Screenshot]

## Breaking Changes

If this PR introduces breaking changes, please describe them:

- [ ] No breaking changes
- [ ] Breaking changes - describe below:

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published
- [ ] I have updated the CHANGELOG.md (if applicable)
- [ ] I have followed the Code of Conduct

Any additional context, notes, or considerations for reviewers:
